name: Guvec or Turlu- Vegetable Casserole
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 180°C (350°F). Cooking time: 1-1/2 to 2 hours.
1. Remove the stem from the eggplants and wash them well. Peel off 1 cm (1/2 inch) strips of skin lengthwise, creating a striped effect on both long and oval eggplants.
2. Cut the eggplants into 1 cm (1/2 inch) slices; quarter the oval eggplants. Place the eggplant pieces on a tray and sprinkle generously with salt. Let them sit for 30 minutes, then pat dry with paper towels.
3. Trim the zucchini and cut into 4 cm (1-1/2 inch) pieces. Remove stems and seeds from the peppers and quarter. Wash the okra and, if desired, soak in vinegar to remove slime; drain thoroughly.
4. If needed, slit the green beans (French cut). Slice the tomatoes.
5. Heat half the oil in a frying pan and fry the eggplants until lightly browned. Transfer to a plate without draining excess fat.
6. Add the remaining oil to the pan, add sliced onions, and fry gently until transparent. Stir in garlic and cook for 1 minute. Remove the pan from heat.
7. In a casserole dish, layer the prepared eggplant, zucchini, peppers, and beans. Spread the onion mixture over the vegetables and cover with tomato slices. Sprinkle with salt, pepper, and parsley.
8. Repeat steps 7 until all ingredients are used, reserving some tomato slices and parsley for garnish.
9. Place the okra on top if using and spread any remaining oil from the eggplant.
10. Cover the casserole dish and bake in a moderate oven for 1 to 1-1/2 hours, or until vegetables are tender. Serve hot as an accompaniment with roast or grilled meats or poultry. This dish can also be served on its own as a light meal, accompanied by bread and peynir (feta) cheese.
